meaning: n.津贴,补助,宽容,允许vt.定量供应
examples: Do you get an allowance for clothing?
She gets an allowance for taking care of Amy.
The budget made allowance for inflation.
"You put bigger constraints on your kid's allowance than they do on their grants, " says Lathram.
Once an airline exceed its allowance, it has to pay to buy extra permits.
